fuelGuide.title: Quick guide for methane and propane
fuelGuide.intro: The notes below are general guidance. The right setup still depends on the engine, luggage space, and how much you drive.
fuelGuide.note: Before making a final choice, review engine condition, expected mileage, and trunk space with the installers.

sections.title: Methane (CNG)
automaticItems: Cuts fuel costs significantly for vehicles with high mileage.
With proper tuning, smooth and stable driving can be preserved on the Automatic program.
Works well for daily commuting and longer routes where efficiency matters.
manualItems: Gas adaptation and control are usually simpler on the Manual program.
Fuel savings stay noticeable even under load.
Service and diagnostics are often easier to manage.

sections.title: Propane (LPG)
automaticItems: The system and cylinder are often lighter, which helps with packaging.
Feels smooth and comfortable in city driving on the Automatic program.
A practical option for shorter everyday routes.
manualItems: Often gives a good balance between installation cost and daily usability.
Practical for short and mixed driving routes.
Maintenance costs remain manageable on many vehicles.
